disclaimer: This is a diagnostic news score overlay. It is not investment advice, market action guidance, execution guidance, or instructions for changing holdings.
freshness_decay_note: Scores use configurable freshness decay so older classified items have less influence than newer classified items.

high_severity_items.summary: Kansas City Fed President Schmid warns that oil price shocks may not be transitory, adding to inflation concerns and reinforcing hawkish monetary policy stance.
high_severity_items.title: Kansas Fed Pres Warns Oil Price Shock Might Not Be Transitory

high_severity_items.summary: The Iran war and disruption of the Strait of Hormuz have removed ~20% of global LNG supply, causing price surges and boosting US LNG exports to Asia. This benefits US energy dominance but raises geopolitical and inflation risks.
high_severity_items.title: America’s LNG Boom Is Real — But China Is Planning Beyond It

high_severity_items.summary: China's oil buying pause may end, worsening the global oil crisis and increasing price pressures.
high_severity_items.title: China’s Oil Buying Pause Won’t Last Forever

high_severity_items.summary: Oil prices drop nearly 10% on expectations of extended ceasefire and partial reopening of Strait of Hormuz, reducing geopolitical risk and easing inflation pressure.
high_severity_items.title: Oil Prices Suffer Biggest Weekly Collapse in Two Months

high_severity_items.summary: Cuba's energy crisis worsens due to US blockade and reduced Venezuelan fuel supply, leading to blackouts and economic strain; the government turns to solar power as a potential solution.
high_severity_items.title: Cuba Bets on Solar Power as Energy Crisis Deepens

high_severity_items.summary: Attacks on multiple vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical oil chokepoint, highlight geopolitical risks and potential supply disruptions.
high_severity_items.title: Chevron CEO: Multiple Ships Attacked In Strait of Hormuz
